name: Saute of Chicken Breasts with Coconut Milk and Herbs
recipeIngredient: 2 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves, tenderloins separated
Half a lime or lemon (seeds removed)
1 small scallion (green onion), trimmed and finely sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Between sheets of plastic wrap, pound the thicker side of the chicken breasts to make them more even in thickness and season lightly with salt.
2. Heat the sesame oil in a skillet over moderate heat. Add the chicken breasts and tenderloins.
3. Saute the chicken without disturbing the pieces for 4 to 5 minutes, or until the top is opaque (tenderloins may take only 2 to 3 minutes per side).
4. Turn the chicken over and saute undisturbed again for another 4 to 5 minutes, or until the interior is cooked through.
5. Remove the chicken to a plate and discard any fat from the skillet. Squeeze the juice from the lime into the skillet and scrape up any coagulated juices.
6. Add the coconut milk, bring to a simmer. Remove the skillet from the heat and whisk in the scallions and cilantro; season to taste with salt and dried red pepper flakes.
Yield: 2 servings
